name: Meat Shield
flavor: Why stop a blade when you can stand someone else in front of it? A yank of the wire, and the nearest enemy becomes a very reluctant barricade.
duration: Until the start of your next turn
description: You haul on the String fixed to a creature you have a String attached to within range, dragging its body where you want it and pinning it there as a living barricade. The target makes a Strength saving throw against your {{trick save DC|8 + proficiency bonus + your trick ability modifier}}. On a failure, you drag it up to 15 feet — through spaces it could normally move through, without provoking opportunity attacks — to an unoccupied space of your choice, where it is restrained until the start of your next turn. While it is pinned this way its body is a shield: any creature has half cover against an attack or effect whose path passes through the meat shield's space. On a success, the wire snaps — the String detaches and nothing else happens. You must already have a String attached to the target to use this trick.
sheetSummary: Bonus action, 60 ft, cooldown 3. Requires a String on the target. Str save. Fail: drag it up to 15 ft (no opportunity attacks) to a space you choose; it is restrained until the start of your next turn and its body grants half cover to anyone behind it. Success: the String detaches, no effect.
